hero.pillText: Schulnetz, OdaOrg, and more — in one app
hero.sub: Schuly brings your grades, schedule, agenda, and absences together in one place. Built for students who want quick access to the school data that matters — on iOS, Android, and the web. Schulnetz and OdaOrg are already supported, with more systems on the way.

features.description: Schuly is built mobile-first — fast, clear, and focused on what matters.

grades.title: Grades & analytics
grades.description: Keep track of your performance at any time. Schuly presents grades, averages, and trends in a clear, easy-to-read way.

agenda.title: Agenda & absences
agenda.description: Schedule, exams, and absences sync automatically and are laid out for comfortable use on mobile devices.

schulnetz.title: Schulnetz, OdaOrg, and more
schulnetz.description: Schulnetz and OdaOrg work today. Thanks to the modular architecture, more systems can be integrated through a backend plugin, with no changes to the app.

multi.title: Multiple schools, one app
multi.description: Add every school you attend — past, present, or future. Switch between them in a single tap while your data stays cleanly separated.

design.title: Modern design
design.description: A clean, modern interface with dark mode and a consistent experience across every platform.

platforms.title: iOS · Android · Web
platforms.description: A shared codebase delivers a native experience on every device — with no compromise on performance or usability.

oidc.title: Secure sign-in
oidc.description: Authentication goes through your school's existing identity provider. Schuly never handles passwords and stores no credentials.

fast.title: Fast and reliable
fast.description: Aggressive caching, optimized data handling, and a modern architecture keep load times short and the app responsive.

screenshots.title: A look inside the app.
screenshots.description: Schuly is built for everyday use. The information that matters is right where you expect it — no detours.

alts.start: Schuly start page — grades and agenda at a glance
alts.agenda: Schuly agenda page — schedule and upcoming exams
alts.grades: Schuly grades page — subject averages and trend chart
alts.absences: Schuly absences page — excused and unexcused list
alts.account: Schuly account page — profile and multi-school switcher

faq.title: Frequently asked questions.
faq.description: What Schuly is, what it isn't, and how it fits alongside the school system you already use.

items.a: Schuly is a free, open-source app for students. It adds a modern, user-friendly interface for grades, schedule, agenda, and absences on top of the school systems your school already uses — and lets you manage multiple schools in one app. iOS, Android, and Web.
Currently Schulnetz (via the Schulware plugin) and OdaOrg, with Schulnetz being the most-used integration. Each system is wrapped by a backend plugin, so more can be added without changing the app.

items.q: Is Schuly an official part of Schulnetz?
items.a: No. Schuly is an independent open-source project and is not affiliated with Centerboard AG, your school, or any other company or institution.
Nothing. The app, the backend, and the plugin SDK are fully open source and available on GitHub at github.com/schulydev.

The backend runs on IONOS in Germany; your data stays within the EU/EEA, with no transfer to third countries. This website is served via Cloudflare Pages, which processes only your IP address for delivery.

items.q: Does Schuly know my school password?
items.a: No. Your Schuly account runs on an external OIDC provider (Pocket ID by default, configurable). Schulnetz uses its own OAuth flow, so no password ever reaches us. OdaOrg currently uses a username and password, which are sent only to the OdaOrg plugin and stored only in its isolated database — never read by, or shared with, the rest of the system.

items.q: Can my school system be integrated?
items.a: Yes. The backend loads plugins that wrap a school system — an API wrapper where an interface exists, otherwise a scraper. Once installed, the system appears in the app's "Add school" flow. Plugins are developed against the Schuly plugin SDK on GitHub.

items.q: I don't want to trust Schuly with my data. What can I do?
items.a: An understandable position. The entire source code — app, backend, plugins, SDK, and this website — is publicly available on GitHub at github.com/schulydev. You can review every line, verify how your data is handled, and run the backend yourself if you want full control. The app can be pointed at your own backend.

items.q: Is Schuly open source? Can I contribute?
items.a: Yes. Every component lives on GitHub at github.com/schulydev. Issues labelled "bug" or "feature" are the easiest place to start. Contributions from the community are always welcome.

download.title: Ready to get started?
download.description: Schuly is free, open source, and available on every major platform.
